pattern: Scanning left to right and stopping at the first character whose second occurrence we hit gives the earliest-completing duplicate in a single pass.
hints: The winner is decided by whose second occurrence comes first, so process characters in order.
Keep a set of letters already seen; the first time you revisit one, you have the answer.
Iterate over the string, return immediately the first character already present in your seen set.

edge_inputs.description: c completes its pair before a or b
repeat only at the very end
earlier letter appears more but pairs later

stress.description: random lowercase strings guaranteed to have a repeat
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = max(2, min(size, 100))
    s = [rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(n - 1)]
    # ensure at least one duplicate by repeating an existing char
    s.append(rng.choice(s))
    rng.shuffle(s)
    return [''.join(s)]
stress.note: shuffle still leaves a duplicate so a valid answer always exists
